seed_source: brain.md v2.0 COMMON FIX PATTERNS section

sidebar_text_change.description: Change text or label in the sidebar
diagnostic_steps: Identify which sidebar section needs change (Rank By, Pillars, Filter by Lab, License, Score Range)
Open index.html around lines 430-465 (sidebar section headers)
Find the exact text to change
common_fixes: Edit HTML directly in sidebar section
If it's a section header with .prominent class, preserve the class

lab_filter_change.description: Reorder or change the lab filter
diagnostic_steps: Open index.html, find buildLabFilter() function (~line 1022)
Check TOP_LABS array â controls pin order
Verify .lab-divider placement between top 6 and rest
common_fixes: Edit TOP_LABS array order
Add/remove labs from pinned list
Verify divider still renders correctly

diagnostic_steps: Open index.html, find openV2Modal() (~line 1100)
Check if rank is using live scores[last] calculation or stored model.rank
NEVER trust stored .rank â trf uses 999 sentinel, tragents has stale ranks
common_fixes: Ensure rank computed as: count models with higher scores[last] + 1
If current score is null or 0, show N/A

scores_not_updating.description: Scores not updating on the live site
diagnostic_steps: Check status.json â did the DDP actually run?
Check the relevant *-data.json dates array â what's the latest date?
Check git log â was the data file pushed?
Check if cron ran (ddp.log)
Check if Mac was awake at 4AM
common_fixes: If DDP didn't run: check daily_runner.py, check cron with crontab -l
If data exists but not pushed: run git push manually
If data is stale: run the specific DDP manually

diagnostic_steps: Check scout-briefing.json exists and has content
Verify keys: top_stories[], stats.total_scraped, stats.passed_filter, narrative_brief
Check mission-control.html JS reads exact keys (ai_verdict, category_label for badges)
common_fixes: Fix key mismatch between scout output and HTML reader
Re-run Content Scout if data is missing

css_tweak.description: CSS tweak â color, size, spacing, alignment
diagnostic_steps: Check styles.css FIRST (global styles)
Then check the specific HTML file's <style> block at top
Identify if issue is parent container or child element
Check media queries for viewport-specific behavior
common_fixes: Edit styles.css for global changes
Edit page-specific <style> block for page-only changes
For alignment: check parent flex/grid container first (80% of alignment issues are parent problems)

nav_link_change.description: Add or remove a navigation link
diagnostic_steps: Determine if change should be site-wide or page-specific
If site-wide: edit nav-v2.js
If page-specific: edit that page's nav block
common_fixes: Edit nav-v2.js for global nav changes
Edit specific HTML page's nav block for one-page changes

diagnostic_steps: Check for duplicate agent processes (409 Telegram conflict)
Check for rate limits (429 errors)
Check if there are merge conflicts (pull --rebase first)
common_fixes: Kill all duplicate processes, restart clean
Agent has retry logic: 15s â 30s â 60s backoff
Always git pull --rebase before push

alignment_issue.description: Visual alignment issues â elements misaligned, spacing off
diagnostic_steps: Identify viewport (mobile, tablet, desktop)
Check parent flex/grid container properties
Check child element margin/padding
Check viewport-specific media queries
common_fixes: Add align-items: center to parent flex container
Use gap instead of margin between children
Check all 3 viewports after fix (375px, 768px, 1440px)
